title: Star Wars Adventures 3: The Hostage Princess
content:
{{Top|rwm|imp|italics=1}} {{Otheruses|Star Wars Adventures 3}} {{Book |image=[[File:Adventures03-Book.jpg]] |title=''Star Wars Adventures 3:
The Hostage Princess'' |author=[[Ryder Windham]] |cover artist= |illustrator= |editor= |publisher=[[Scholastic]] |early release= |release date=[[December]] [[2002]]''Star Wars Adventures 3: The Hostage Princess'' was released in a bundle with [[Star Wars Adventures Magazine 3|''Star Wars Adventure Magazine'' 3]], which was released in [[December]] [[2002]] |intl release= |rereleased= |media type=Paperback |pages=90 |isbn=0439458811 |isbn2= |isbn3= |timeline=[[23 BBY/Legends|23 BBY]]{{CSWECite|I|151|Cradossk}} dates the events of the [[Star Wars Adventures (Scholastic)|''Star Wars Adventures'']] series to a year before the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|First Battle of Geonosis]], which ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates to [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]]. |series=[[Star Wars Adventures (Scholastic)|''Star Wars Adventures'' (Scholastic)]] |preceded by=[[Star Wars Adventures 2: The Cavern of Screaming Skulls|''The Cavern of Screaming Skulls'']] |followed by=[[Star Wars Adventures 4: Jango Fett vs. the Razor Eaters|''Jango Fett vs. the Razor Eaters'']] }} '''''Star Wars Adventures 3: The Hostage Princess''''' was the third installment of [[Scholastic]]'s [[Star Wars Adventures (Scholastic)|''Star Wars Adventures'']] series. It consisted of a young reader novel and an accompanying game book for use with the story, and was sent out with [[Star Wars Adventures Magazine 3|''Star Wars Adventures Magazine'' 3]] in [[December]] [[2002]]. ==Publisher's summary== [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]] and [[Kit Fisto/Legends|Kit Fisto]] investigate the sudden siege of a [[Fondor/Legends|Fondor]] [[Fondor Spaceport|spaceport]] by [[Droid Control Army|droid forces]]. The capture of a [[Nallastian]] [[Calvaria|noble]] leads the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] on a [[Mission to Fondor (Separatist Crisis)|pursuit]] [...] they must act quickly to save the life of the captive [[Prince/Legends|princess]]. ==Plot summary== {{Plot}} ==Continuity== [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Bultar Swan/Legends|Bultar Swan's]] comment to 18-year-old [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] at the [[Skull Queen]]'s [[Skull Queen's fortress|fortress]] that "[[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] don't have [[Dream/Legends|nightmares]]"''[[Star Wars Adventures 3: The Hostage Princess]]'' presages Anakin's identical words to [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Amidala]] as he stands on the east balcony of the [[Naberrie family/Legends|Naberrie family's]] [[Varykino/Legends|Varykino]] lodge (in both ''Star Wars'': Episode II ''Attack of the Clones''{{'}} [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones (novelization)|novel]][[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones (novelization)|''Star Wars'': Episode II ''Attack of the Clones'' novelization]] and [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|screenplay]]{{Film|II}}) that appear to directly quote Swan from Skywalker's [[Skull Queen's fortress#Disturbing dreams|memory]] of their [[Skull Queen's fortress#No nightmares|discussion]] about Jedi dreams during their [[Mission to Fondor (Separatist Crisis)|mission to Fondor]] some months earlier. ==Media== ===Cover gallery=== File:Adventures03-Book.jpg|Novel cover File:Adventures03-GameBook.jpg|Game Book cover
